raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, I received a {$1000.00} XXXX order from XXXX XXXX. The order was defective and incomplete ( missing baskets, unsafe front fork ). I reported this to the merchant the same day. 

On XX/XX/year>, I attempted to return the order under their advertised 30-day free trial and under North Carolina law . Instead of honoring this, XXXX demanded {$200.00} in fees and later shipped one basket weeks after my rejection, attempting to moot my return.

I filed a dispute with Citi, as well as complaints with the XXXX  and the NC DOJ. XXXX sent a factually incorrect response to the XXXX  and ignored the NC DOJ entirely. Citi repeatedly granted XXXX 30-day extensions when the merchant ignored them, finally responding after more than 90 days.

Despite my supplying documentary proofincluding XXXX  tracking showing the bike was returned to XXXX on XX/XX/year>, using their own label, and the merchants own email dated XX/XX/year>, admitting receiptCiti closed the dispute with the statement the merchandise is still in your possession. This determination is factually false. 

I escalated and requested Citi provide the merchants evidencethe documentation XXXX submitted as the basis for this adverse decision. Citi refused this request during my first escalation in XX/XX/year>, and again after the adverse determination in late XXXX and XX/XX/year>. On XX/XX/year>, I explicitly requested Citi preserve all records relating to this dispute. 

Citis refusal to properly credit my account despite clear evidence violates the Truth in Lending Act ( Regulation Z, 12 CFR 1026.13 ). I am seeking reversal of the {$1000.00} charge, reimbursement of interest accrued during these delays, and preservation of all records for further action.
